content:
Hi Kilgetty

Are you closing the Thunderbird app via the recent apps list. This would stop the push service running in the background. An ongoing notification is displayed while the service is running.

You should turther enable the app to run in the background and exclude it from Android's battery optimisations. Goto android settings > apps > app name > battery: Allow background activity
creator: platform34

content:
No, there's no integration supported.

Feel free to make the suggestion on Mozilla connect where users can vote for your idea. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/idb-p/ideas/label-name/thunderbird%20android
creator: platform34

content:
Thunderbird for Android doesn't have it's own address book or method to sync with the desktop.

An address book has been requested by the community on mozilla connect. You can show support by voting for the idea (linked below) or add your own.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/an-address-book-in-thunderbird-for-android/idi-p/84952
creator: platform34

content:

content:
Hi @Wilsonians

Can you provide details about the issue your experiencing? Please share any error information displayed by the app.

  • Are you unable to add the account?
  • Is the issue with a specific feature or function your trying to configure?
  • Who is your email provider?

Note that Thunderbird for Android does not support using oAuth authentication with pop3 accounts. You should create an app password via your providers webmail if this situation is applicable to you.
creator: platform34

content:
Hi,

I think you just need to go into the Settings, in the list of accounts, select the one to be removed, then in the triple-dot menu in the top right, select Remove Account.
